bots.name: Googlebot
bots.author: Google LLC
bots.summary: Google's primary web crawler for indexing pages in Google Search. Completely legitimate when verified via reverse DNS. Many impersonators exist — always verify before trusting. Site operators can control access via robots.txt.

recommendation.text: Allow verified Googlebot. Verify via rDNS: IP → hostname ending in .googlebot.com or .google.com → forward DNS back to same IP. Block any Googlebot UA from non-Google IP ranges immediately.

iocs.note: Can be spoofed — verify via rDNS
Legitimate Googlebot always respects crawl directives

timeline.event: Googlebot first deployed to index the early web for BackRub.
Google announces Googlebot now renders JavaScript for indexing.
Googlebot moves to evergreen Chromium-based rendering engine.
Increased frequency of smartphone Googlebot for mobile-first indexing.

behaviors.text: Performs reverse DNS verification — rDNS resolves to *.googlebot.com.
IMPERSONATORS: Many malicious bots fake Googlebot UA. Always verify via rDNS.

mitigation.title: Verify via Reverse DNS
mitigation.desc:
Run: host <IP>
Confirm hostname ends in .googlebot.com or .google.com, then forward-resolve to confirm match.

mitigation.title: Block Impersonators
mitigation.desc: Create server rules to block any IP claiming Googlebot UA that fails rDNS verification.

mitigation.title: Control via robots.txt
mitigation.desc: Use Crawl-delay and Disallow directives to control crawl scope and frequency.

detection.signature: User-Agent contains 'Googlebot' AND rDNS resolves to *.googlebot.com AND forward DNS matches source IP
detection.yara:
rule Googlebot_UserAgent {
    meta:
        description = "Detects Googlebot user-agent"
        author = "OSINTrix Bot Wiki"
        date = "2025-01"
    strings:
        $ua1 = "Googlebot/2.1" ascii nocase
        $ua2 = "Googlebot-Image" ascii nocase
    condition:
        any of ($ua*)
}
detection.apache:
# .htaccess — Block Googlebot from non-Google IPs
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{HTTP_USER_AGENT} [Gg]ooglebot [NC]
RewriteCond %{REMOTE_ADDR} !^66\.249\.
RewriteCond %{REMOTE_ADDR} !^64\.233\.
RewriteCond %{REMOTE_ADDR} !^74\.125\.
RewriteRule ^ - [F,L]
</IfModule>
detection.cloudflare:
# Cloudflare WAF — Block Googlebot Impersonators
# Expression:
(http.user_agent contains "Googlebot" and not ip.src in {66.249.64.0/19 66.249.80.0/20 64.233.160.0/19 74.125.0.0/16 209.85.128.0/17})
# Action: Block
detection.modsecurity:
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:User-Agent "@contains Googlebot" \
    "id:9001001,phase:1,deny,status:403,\
    msg:'Googlebot Impersonation',tag:'BOT_IMPERSONATION',chain"
    SecRule REMOTE_ADDR "!@ipMatch 66.249.64.0/19,66.249.80.0/20,64.233.160.0/19"

bots.name: AhrefsBot
bots.author: Ahrefs Pte Ltd
bots.summary: Commercial web crawler that builds Ahrefs' backlink database. Extremely aggressive crawl rate — often top-5 globally by volume. Provides no direct SEO benefit to crawled sites. Respects robots.txt. Many operators block it to save bandwidth, especially on shared hosting.

recommendation.text: Block or rate-limit if bandwidth is a concern. AhrefsBot respects robots.txt. Blocking has no SEO penalty. Consider allowing if you actively use Ahrefs products.

iocs.value: Very high crawl frequency — millions req/day

timeline.event: Ahrefs launches AhrefsBot to build backlink database.
Becomes one of top 5 most active crawlers globally.

behaviors.text: Extremely aggressive crawl rate — significant bandwidth impact on smaller hosts.

mitigation.title: Block via robots.txt
mitigation.desc:
User-agent: AhrefsBot
Disallow: /

Simplest method. Takes effect within 24–48h.

mitigation.title: Server-level UA Block
mitigation.desc: Block 'AhrefsBot' UA string at Nginx/Apache/Cloudflare for immediate effect.

mitigation.title: Rate Limiting
mitigation.desc:
If you want Ahrefs data but lower frequency:
Crawl-delay: 10

detection.yara:
rule AhrefsBot_Crawler {
    meta:
        description = "Detects AhrefsBot SEO crawler"
        author = "OSINTrix Bot Wiki"
        classification = "gray"
    strings:
        $ua1 = "AhrefsBot" ascii nocase
        $ua2 = "ahrefs.com/robot" ascii nocase
    condition:
        any of ($ua*)
}
detection.apache:
# .htaccess — Block AhrefsBot
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{HTTP_USER_AGENT} AhrefsBot [NC]
RewriteRule .* - [F,L]
</IfModule>
detection.cloudflare:
# Cloudflare WAF — Block AhrefsBot
# Expression:
(http.user_agent contains "AhrefsBot")
# Action: Block or Challenge
detection.modsecurity:
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:User-Agent "@contains AhrefsBot" \
    "id:9002001,phase:1,deny,status:403,\
    msg:'AhrefsBot SEO Crawler Blocked',tag:'SEO_CRAWLER'"

bots.name: Scrapy Generic Scraper
bots.author: Unknown / Various
bots.summary: Scrapy is a legitimate open-source Python framework, but unidentified generic Scrapy bots in logs typically indicate unauthorized scraping — harvesting prices, emails, listings, or copyrighted content. Default Scrapy UA in access logs is almost always unauthorized. Operators using Scrapy legitimately customize their user-agent.

recommendation.text: Block the default Scrapy UA immediately. Implement rate limiting and honeypot traps as secondary defenses. Header anomaly detection catches rotating scrapers.

iocs.note: Default Scrapy UA — almost always unauthorized

iocs.value: High request rate to structured data pages
iocs.note: Price lists, search results, product IDs

timeline.event: Scrapy open-sourced; immediately adopted for unauthorized scraping.
Rise of scraping-as-a-service platforms built on Scrapy.
Residential proxy services enable Scrapy deployments evading IP blocks.
AI training data harvesting via Scrapy surges significantly.

behaviors.text: Often ignores robots.txt — check logs for crawl of Disallowed paths.
High concurrency — dozens of simultaneous requests causing load spikes.
Systematic URL enumeration — crawls paginated results or sequential product IDs.
Scrapy itself is a legitimate tool — block the behavior and default UA.

mitigation.title: Block Default Scrapy UA
mitigation.desc: Block any request with 'Scrapy' in User-Agent immediately. Legitimate users always set custom UAs.

mitigation.title: Rate Limiting
mitigation.desc: Limit to 60 req/min per IP. Return 429 Too Many Requests. Use Nginx limit_req or Cloudflare Rate Limiting.

mitigation.title: Honeypot Links
mitigation.desc: Add hidden links (display:none) to pages. Any bot following them is definitively a scraper. Auto-ban IP.

mitigation.title: Header Anomaly Detection
mitigation.desc: Block requests missing Accept-Language or Sec-Fetch-Site. Script scrapers rarely include these correctly.

mitigation.title: CAPTCHA on High-Value Pages
mitigation.desc: Deploy Cloudflare Turnstile or hCaptcha on pricing pages and data-rich search results.

detection.signature: User-Agent contains 'Scrapy' OR missing Accept-Language AND high request rate from single IP
detection.yara:
rule Scrapy_Scraper {
    meta:
        description = "Detects Scrapy-based scraper by default UA"
        author = "OSINTrix Bot Wiki"
        classification = "malicious"
    strings:
        $ua1 = "Scrapy/" ascii nocase
        $ua2 = "scrapy.org" ascii nocase
    condition:
        any of ($ua*)
}
detection.sigma:
title: Scrapy Web Scraper Activity
status: stable
description: Detects Scrapy framework usage indicating potential unauthorized scraping
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-user-agent|contains: 'Scrapy/'
  condition: selection
level: high
tags:
  - attack.collection
  - attack.t1119
detection.apache:
# .htaccess — Block Scrapy
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{HTTP_USER_AGENT} Scrapy [NC]
RewriteRule .* - [F,L]
RewriteCond %{HTTP_USER_AGENT} Python-urllib [NC]
RewriteRule .* - [F,L]
</IfModule>
detection.cloudflare:
# Cloudflare WAF — Block Scrapy
# Expression:
(http.user_agent contains "Scrapy")
# Action: Block

# Also add Rate Limiting rule:
# Path: /products/* or /search
# Threshold: 60 req/min
# Action: Block
detection.modsecurity:
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:User-Agent "@rx [Ss]crapy" \
    "id:9003001,phase:1,deny,status:403,\
    msg:'Scrapy Scraper Detected',tag:'BOT_MALICIOUS',severity:'HIGH'"

# Header anomaly — missing Accept-Language
SecRule &REQUEST_HEADERS:Accept-Language "@eq 0" \
    "id:9003002,phase:1,pass,setvar:tx.bot_score=+2"

SecRule TX:BOT_SCORE "@gt 3" \
    "id:9003003,phase:1,deny,status:403,msg:'Bot Anomaly Detected'"

bots.name: Bingbot
bots.author: Microsoft Corporation
bots.summary: Microsoft's web crawler for Bing search. Like Googlebot, it should be verified via reverse DNS before being trusted. Verified Bingbot is completely benign. Blocking it removes your site from Bing search results.

recommendation.text: Allow verified Bingbot. Verify: IP should rDNS to *.search.msn.com, forward-resolve to same IP. Block any Bingbot UA from non-Microsoft IP ranges.

timeline.event: MSNBot succeeded by Bingbot as Microsoft launches Bing.
Activity increases following Microsoft OpenAI/Bing integration.

behaviors.text: Bingbot impersonation is common — always verify.

mitigation.title: Verify via Reverse DNS
mitigation.desc: host <IP> → must resolve to *.search.msn.com → forward resolve back to same IP.

mitigation.title: Block Impersonators
mitigation.desc: Block Bingbot UA from non-Microsoft ASN (AS8075, AS8068). Impersonators use Bingbot UA to appear legitimate.

mitigation.title: Use Bing Webmaster Tools
mitigation.desc: Monitor crawl stats and adjust crawl rate at webmaster.bing.com.

detection.apache:
# .htaccess — Block Bingbot from non-Microsoft IPs
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{HTTP_USER_AGENT} bingbot [NC]
RewriteCond %{REMOTE_ADDR} !^40\.77\.167\.
RewriteCond %{REMOTE_ADDR} !^207\.46\.
RewriteRule ^ - [F,L]
</IfModule>
detection.cloudflare:
# Cloudflare WAF — Block Bingbot Impersonators
# Expression:
(http.user_agent contains "bingbot" and not ip.src in {40.77.167.0/24 207.46.0.0/16 157.54.0.0/15 65.52.0.0/14})
# Action: Block

bots.name: GPTBot / ChatGPT-User
bots.author: OpenAI
bots.summary: OpenAI operates two distinct crawlers: GPTBot harvests content for AI model training, and ChatGPT-User fetches real-time content for ChatGPT's browsing. GPTBot is controversial — it uses published content for commercial AI training without compensation. Many publishers now block it by default. Both respect robots.txt.

recommendation.text: Block GPTBot if you don't want your content used for AI training (no SEO penalty). Allow ChatGPT-User if you want ChatGPT to cite and link to your content. Both respect robots.txt opt-out.

timeline.event: OpenAI announces GPTBot with robots.txt opt-out.
Major publishers (NYT, CNN) begin blocking GPTBot.
OpenAI lawsuit with NYT reveals scale of content harvesting.
Reddit, Wikipedia restrict AI crawlers amid licensing disputes.

behaviors.text: Respects robots.txt — add 'User-agent: GPTBot / Disallow: /' to opt out.
Content used for commercial AI training without compensation or consent.
Does not exploit vulnerabilities or exfiltrate user PII.

mitigation.title: Block GPTBot via robots.txt
mitigation.desc:
User-agent: GPTBot
Disallow: /

Official opt-out. Takes effect within days.

mitigation.title: Differentiate Crawlers
mitigation.desc: Block GPTBot (training) but allow ChatGPT-User (citations/traffic). Use separate robots.txt rules.

mitigation.title: Server-Level Block
mitigation.desc: Block 'GPTBot' UA at Nginx/Apache/Cloudflare for immediate effect.

detection.yara:
rule OpenAI_Crawlers {
    meta:
        description = "Detects OpenAI crawlers (GPTBot, ChatGPT-User)"
        author = "OSINTrix Bot Wiki"
        classification = "gray"
    strings:
        $ua1 = "GPTBot" ascii nocase
        $ua2 = "ChatGPT-User" ascii nocase
    condition:
        any of ($ua*)
}
detection.apache:
# .htaccess — Block GPTBot
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{HTTP_USER_AGENT} GPTBot [NC]
RewriteRule .* - [F,L]
</IfModule>
detection.cloudflare:
# Cloudflare WAF — Block GPTBot
# Expression:
(http.user_agent contains "GPTBot")
# Action: Block

# Note: Cloudflare Security > Bots has
# 'AI Scrapers and Crawlers' toggle in newer plans
detection.modsecurity:
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:User-Agent "@rx (GPTBot|ChatGPT-User)" \
    "id:9005001,phase:1,deny,status:403,\
    msg:'OpenAI AI Training Crawler Blocked',tag:'BOT_AI_CRAWLER'"
